text:
Once upon an autumn time in a quiet town, a mysterious tale began. There were whispers of an old oak tree that leaned peculiar ways. It was said that beneath its broad roots lay secrets whispered only by the wind itself.

A young girl named Lily lived alone at the edge of town. She loved wandering through the forest claiming just the slightest nibble was her favourite kind. One crisp afternoon, she decided to search for just such a thing beneath the oldest tree.

As she sat quietly, listening to their secrets, an ant scampered up her arm. It paused. Did it sense something special? Just as she pondered this strange feeling, a small leaf fell ominously. Perhaps there was something sinister in the oak?

Suddenly, the branch above fell. An owl hooted eerily. Concerned but intrigued, Lily crept closer. Underneath the gnarled trunk was a tiny skull.

Curiosity overcame fear. She gently cradled it. Who was this creature?
